news.headline: Novo Nordisk Shares Encouraging Long-Term Data From Hemophilia Program
news.summary: Novo Nordisk presented interim results from the open-label Phase 3 FRONTIER4 extension study of denecimig (Mim8) in 426 hemophilia A patients aged one and older, showing sustained reductions in joint pain and lower treatment burden. Denecimig remains under FDA review following a Biologics License Application submitted in September 2025, underscoring Novo's push to diversify beyond its GLP-1 franchise.

news.headline: Wegovy (semaglutide) label updated in Singapore with higher dose supported by STEP UP evidence
news.summary: Novo updated Wegovy's Singapore label to include a higher 7.2 mg injectable dose backed by the 72-week STEP UP trial, in which mean weight loss reached 21% and roughly one in three patients lost at least 25% of body weight. The data extends Novo's higher-dose obesity data package as it defends its efficacy positioning against rivals.

news.headline: Novo Nordisk withdraws CagriSema device-comparison study, nudging investor focus back to core obesity assets
news.summary: Novo Nordisk withdrew a Phase 3 study that was comparing two injectable-device versions of CagriSema, its next-generation GLP-1/amylin obesity combination. The withdrawal slightly clouds the near-term development picture for CagriSema even as the core FDA review of the drug, filed in December 2025, continues toward an expected H2 2026 decision.

news.headline: Novo Nordisk Korea merges obesity and diabetes divisions in executive reshuffle
news.summary: Novo Nordisk's Korean unit merged its previously separate obesity and diabetes business divisions and reshuffled senior executives, a move seen as an effort to speed decision-making after Eli Lilly's Mounjaro overtook Wegovy in the domestic injectable obesity market. The restructuring underscores intensifying competitive pressure on Novo outside its core U.S. and European markets.

news.headline: Medicare obesity drug GLP-1 coverage starting July 1
news.summary: Eligible Medicare beneficiaries gained access to Wegovy (injection and pill) for a $50/month copay from July 1, 2026 via the Medicare GLP-1 Bridge program, running through end-2027. Novo and Eli Lilly agreed to supply the drugs to CMS at a net price of $245/month, opening a large new reimbursed patient pool ahead of the January 2027 list-price cut.

news.headline: Hacking Group Claims Major Hack of Novo Nordisk and Attempted $25 Million Extortion
news.summary: A cyber-extortion group calling itself FulcrumSec claimed it accessed Novo Nordisk's internal systems for over two months using a compromised GitHub access token and demanded a $25 million ransom, which Novo refused to pay; the group says it is now leaking and shopping stolen source code, trial and employee data. A separate hacker also claimed an earlier breach and a $50 million demand, adding a data-security overhang to the stock.

catalysts.label: H1/Q2 2026 earnings release
catalysts.detail: Financial report for the six months to 30 June 2026, due 5 August 2026 (07:30 CEST); focus on Wegovy pill adoption post-UK launch and Medicare Bridge uptake, underlying obesity-care momentum ex one-off pricing, and any guidance revision.

catalysts.label: Capital Markets Day
catalysts.detail: Novo Nordisk's executive management will present a full review of strategy, operations and financial targets and is expected to unveil new Strategic Aspirations, including expected detail on CagriSema's launch timeline.

catalysts.label: FDA decision on CagriSema
catalysts.detail: Expected FDA verdict on the first once-weekly GLP-1/amylin combination (semaglutide + cagrilintide) for chronic weight management, filed in December 2025 on REDEFINE data. The open-label REDEFINE 4 head-to-head study versus Lilly's tirzepatide showed 23.0% weight loss for CagriSema versus 25.5% for Zepbound, and Novo has since withdrawn a related device-comparison study, adding some uncertainty ahead of this key re-rating catalyst.

catalysts.label: FDA decision on oral semaglutide 25 mg for obesity
catalysts.detail: Supplemental application for higher-dose oral semaglutide 25 mg in obesity under FDA review, with a decision expected by end of 2026 to broaden the oral GLP-1 franchise versus Lilly's orforglipron (Foundayo), which was FDA-approved and launched in April 2026.

catalysts.label: EU launch of Wegovy pill
catalysts.detail: Following the CHMP positive opinion (21 May 2026) recommending EU marketing authorization for oral semaglutide 25 mg, Novo plans to launch the Wegovy pill in the first markets outside the U.S. (likely UK, Germany, Denmark) during H2 2026, following the earlier UAE launch.

catalysts.label: U.S. list-price cut takes effect
catalysts.detail: Wegovy, Ozempic and Rybelsus list prices drop to ~$675/month (roughly -50% / -35%), coinciding with new Medicare-negotiated pricing under the GLP-1 Bridge; a margin headwind but a volume-defense move.

pulse: Novo Nordisk's newsflow remains a tug-of-war between pipeline and franchise progress and lingering overhangs: fresh long-term denecimig hemophilia data and a Singapore Wegovy label update sit alongside the withdrawal of a CagriSema device-comparison study and an unresolved June cyber-extortion incident (FulcrumSec's $25M ransom demand over stolen trial and source-code data), while a Korean divisional restructuring highlights how far Eli Lilly's Mounjaro/orforglipron have eaten into Wegovy's international share. The stock has stabilized in the high-$40s helped by a large ongoing buyback and Medicare's new $50/month GLP-1 Bridge coverage (effective July 1) expanding the reimbursed U.S. patient pool. Attention now turns to the August 5 H1 earnings print, the September 21 Capital Markets Day where new strategic targets and a CagriSema launch timeline are expected, and the pending H2 2026 FDA decision and EU launch for the next-generation obesity portfolio, all set against a scheduled ~50% U.S. list-price cut in January 2027.
